definition: Customer Relationship Management — a system for managing interactions with current and potential customers.
extendedDefinition: CRM software centralizes customer data including contact details, communication history, and purchase records in a single accessible platform. Sales teams use CRMs to track leads, manage deals through a pipeline, and automate follow-up tasks. Marketing and support departments also rely on CRM data to personalize campaigns and resolve tickets efficiently. A well-implemented CRM strategy directly improves customer retention and revenue growth.
